title: Spreadsheets, Python, Reproducibility, and Calculation Checks · Guided Modeling Studio
summary: Turn a hand calculation into a reusable, tested worksheet and small Python model. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a reproducible calculation tool with test cases while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use relative and absolute cell references intentionally in copied formulas.
Add input constraints, units, labels, and audit cells to a spreadsheet model.
Express a unit-aware calculation as readable Python variables, functions, and output.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints

steps.title: Produce a checked model result
steps.instruction: A measured quantity starts at 20 units, changes by −7 units, then by +4 units. Calculate the final quantity.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Preserve the signs and compute 20 − 7 + 4; compare with the starting scale.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Preserve the signs and compute 20 − 7 + 4; compare with the starting scale.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes units.

steps.title: Encode a reproducible check
steps.instruction: Add a evidence_math_beg_reproducible_report_1wf8ro function that computes or traces the changed case, asserts one meaningful invariant, and returns inspectable evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Reproducible Report into observable performance evidence.

content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

stimulus.title: Reproducible Report failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Reproducible Report condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Reproducible Report applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Publish inputs, method, version, results, limitations, and rerun instructions.
